title: Cambridge scraps its most controversial proposed alcohol rule changes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cambridge License Commission dropped proposed rules for earlier last call and 30-minute wait between drink orders.
Public backlash led to reconsideration of draft alcohol regulations following feedback from residents and business owners.
Additional listening sessions with license holders and local entities are planned before formal discussions in August.

title: 3 generations of R.I. family died in Pawtucket accident
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three generations of a Rhode Island family died after their car went into the Seekonk River in Pawtucket.
The victims included a 45-year-old, a 22-year-old, and a 2-year-old girl, identified by family as members of the Gomez family.
Pawtucket Police stated the car's brakes were functioning and no foul play is suspected, calling it a tragic accident.

title: Report: Celtics, Mitchell Robinson agree to three-year deal worth $47.4 million
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Celtics sign Mitchell Robinson to a three-year, $47.4 million contract with a player option for the third year.
Mitchell Robinson joins the Celtics after winning an NBA title with the New York Knicks, where he played since 2018.
The Celtics earlier agreed to a one-year contract with veteran guard Mike Conley Jr., according to reports.

title: Celtics reportedly agree to one-year deal with Mike Conley Jr.
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Boston Celtics signed 38-year-old guard Mike Conley Jr. to a one-year contract, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
Mike Conley Jr., previously with the Timberwolves, has played 19 NBA seasons including with the Jazz and Grizzlies.
The Celtics' offseason moves may hinge on potential changes involving small forward Jaylen Brown's role or trade status.

title: What Revolution players are saying about ex-teammate facing the U.S. in the World Cup Round of 32
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Esmir Bajraktarević, ex-New England Revolution player, will face the U.S. in the World Cup for Bosnia-Herzegovina.
Bajraktarević, raised in the U.S., switched to Bosnia’s national team in 2024, reflecting his family's Bosnian heritage.
Revolution teammates recall Bajraktarević's passion for his Bosnian roots and his impact on the field as a motivated player.

title: Local obituary: Jake Mick, 31, state trooper and father to infant daughter
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Jacob G. Mick, 31, passed away on June 28, 2026, due to a motorcycle accident.
Jake Mick served as a Massachusetts State Trooper and was assigned to the Holden Barracks (C6).
He is survived by his wife Kaila, infant daughter Natalie, and extended family.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in July
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: The Lebanese-inspired Bey restaurant opens in Boston's South End offering a variety of mezze and Lebanese wines.
Sam Adams Upper Deck debuts at Hotel Commonwealth with Boston-lager battered fish and other beer-themed dishes.
Urban Hearth expands its space in Inman Square, offering an 8 to 12 course chef's counter experience.

title: 24-year-old woman dies after falling off jet ski in Mass. pond
author: John Waller
bullets: A 24-year-old woman from Brockton died after falling off a jet ski on Long Pond in Lakeville.
Emergency personnel, including a State Police helicopter and dive teams, found the woman's body at 12:30 a.m. Wednesday.
The incident near Nelson Island and Big Beach is under investigation by Lakeville police.

title: Father facing several charges after 5-year-old son killed, 10-month-old daughter injured in Maine crash
author: John Waller
bullets: Indiana father Dion Campbell faces multiple charges after a crash killed his 5-year-old son in Hancock County, Maine.
Campbell's 10-month-old daughter was injured, and both children were not properly restrained in the vehicle.
The crash, resulting from allegedly driving under the influence, occurred at the Routes 9 and 193 intersection.

title: Family seeks help bringing home Massachusetts man who fell critically ill in Fiji
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Holbrook resident Scott Winslow fell critically ill in Fiji from an infection suspected to be initiated by an insect bite.
Winslow is hospitalized in Fiji with septic shock, while his family lacks insurance to cover the unexpected medical expenses.
A GoFundMe campaign has raised over $24,000 of the $40,000 goal to fund his medical transport back to the U.S.
